content.content:
title:	[Bug]: LLM consistently doesn't see its tool call outputs in next iterations
state:	OPEN
author:	alexhooketh
labels:	bug
comments:	2
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	319
--
### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have searched [existing issues](https://github.com/moltisorg/moltis/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3Abug) and this hasn't been reported yet
- [x] I am using the latest version of Moltis
- [x] If this happened during a chat session, I included as much full session context as possible and redacted secrets

### What happened?

noticing this behavior in pretty much every chat — the LLM seems to not be able to see its tool call outputs when i send a new message to the chat. this makes it confused and think it has hallucinated everything above and i have to verbally calm it down. not just that, when it becomes convinced that it did produce valid data with (as it sees) no tool calls earlier, it starts outright hallucinating the info confidently, rendering chats unusable after just a few messages
i'm using telegram


### Expected behavior

pretty sure that openclaw does include tool calls in the chat history, because i've never encountered this issue (or even the agent saying that it has hallucinated anything, really) on openclaw. i'd expect this behavior to be the same. i believe that it's either a bug or an attempt to save context usage — if the latter, it's certainly an unsuccessful one

### Steps to reproduce

run a moltis agent on telegram. i use gpt-5.2 via codex oauth
ask it to do anything tool-heavy
signs of it not seeing its own tool call outputs are obvious after just a few messages

### Did this happen during a chat session?

None

### Chat session context (if applicable)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0e5b3658-5e60-4a5f-b6a1-5aa73d4806c0)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/913153b0-77c4-49bf-972c-dbdc5474a39f)

### Error messages / logs

```shell
i think that it's rather undesired behavior than a bug
```

### Is this a regression?

No, this never worked

### Last working version

_No response_

### Moltis version

0.10.11

### Component

Agent / LLM providers

### Install method

Pre-built binary

### Operating system

Ubuntu / Debian

### Additional context

_No response_

Yes, this is very broken behavior, which has terrible consequences:

1. agents are extremely dumb
2. this burns through tokens because resubmitted conversation does not match the original one, so prefix caching does not work
--

input.prompt:
I need to understand how chat/conversation history is built and sent to LLM providers in this Rust codebase. The bug is that tool call outputs/results are not included in the chat history when sending to the LLM on subsequent messages.

Search thoroughly for:
1. How chat messages are stored and retrieved (look in crates/gateway, crates/sessions, and related)
2. How the message history is constructed before sending to an LLM provider (look for functions that build the messages array/vec)
3. How tool calls and tool results are represented in the message types
4. Any filtering or transformation of messages that might strip tool results

Key terms to search for: "tool_call", "tool_result", "ToolCall", "ToolResult", "chat_history", "message_log", "messages", "conversation", "history"

Look at the data flow from message storage through to LLM API calls. Focus on where tool results might be dropped.

1→//! Typed message structures for session storage.
     2→//!
     3→//! These types represent the JSON format stored in session JSONL files.
     4→//! They include both LLM-relevant fields (role, content) and metadata
     5→//! fields (created_at, model, provider, tokens, channel).
     6→
     7→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     8→
     9→/// A message stored in a session JSONL file.
    10→///
    11→/// Includes both the LLM-relevant content and metadata for UI display
    12→/// and analytics. The `role` field determines which variant this is.
    13→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    14→#[serde(tag = "role", rename_all = "lowercase")]
    15→pub enum PersistedMessage {
    16→    System {
    17→        content: String,
    18→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    19→        created_at: Option<u64>,
    20→    },
    21→    /// UI-only informational message (not part of LLM prompt history).
    22→    Notice {
    23→        content: String,
    24→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    25→        created_at: Option<u64>,
    26→    },
    27→    User {
    28→        /// Content can be a string (plain text) or array (multimodal).
    29→        content: MessageContent,
    30→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    31→        created_at: Option<u64>,
    32→        /// Relative media path for uploaded user audio (e.g. "media/main/voice-123.webm").
    33→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    34→        audio: Option<String>,
    35→        /// Channel metadata for UI display (e.g., Telegram sender info).
    36→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    37→        channel: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    38→        /// Client-assigned sequence number for ordering diagnostics.
    39→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    40→        seq: Option<u64>,
    41→        /// Agent run ID that processes this message (parent→child link).
    42→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    43→        run_id: Option<String>,
    44→    },
    45→    Assistant {
    46→        content: String,
    47→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    48→        created_at: Option<u64>,
    49→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    50→        model: Option<String>,
    51→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    52→        provider: Option<String>,
    53→        /// Total input tokens spent during this assistant turn.
    54→        #[serde(rename = "inputTokens", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    55→        input_tokens: Option<u32>,
    56→        /// Total output tokens produced during this assistant turn.
    57→        #[serde(rename = "outputTokens", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    58→        output_tokens: Option<u32>,
    59→        #[serde(rename = "durationMs", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    60→        duration_ms: Option<u64>,
    61→        /// Input tokens sent in the final LLM request for this turn.
    62→        #[serde(rename = "requestInputTokens", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    63→        request_input_tokens: Option<u32>,
    64→        /// Output tokens produced in the final LLM request for this turn.
    65→        #[serde(
    66→            rename = "requestOutputTokens",
    67→            skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"
    68→        )]
    69→        request_output_tokens: Option<u32>,
    70→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    71→        tool_calls: Option<Vec<PersistedToolCall>>,
    72→        /// Optional provider reasoning/planning text (not final answer text).
    73→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    74→        reasoning: Option<String>,
    75→        /// Raw provider API payload captured during streaming for debugging.
    76→        #[serde(rename = "llmApiResponse", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    77→        llm_api_response: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    78→        /// Relative media path for TTS audio (e.g. "media/main/run_abc.ogg").
    79→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    80→        audio: Option<String>,
    81→        /// Sequence number matching the user message this responds to.
    82→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    83→        seq: Option<u64>,
    84→        /// Agent run ID linking this response to its parent user message.
    85→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    86→        run_id: Option<String>,
    87→    },
    88→    Tool {
    89→        tool_call_id: String,
    90→        content: String,
    91→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    92→        created_at: Option<u64>,
    93→    },
    94→    /// Tool execution result with structured output (stdout, stderr, exit_code).
    95→    ///
    96→    /// Persisted alongside user/assistant messages so that the UI can
    97→    /// reconstruct exec cards when a session is reloaded.
    98→    #[serde(rename = "tool_result")]
    99→    ToolResult {
   100→        tool_call_id: String,
   101→        tool_name: String,
   102→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   103→        arguments: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   104→        success: bool,
   105→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   106→        result: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   107→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   108→        error: Option<String>,
   109→        /// Provider reasoning/thinking text that preceded this tool call.
   110→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   111→        reasoning: Option<String>,
   112→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   113→        created_at: Option<u64>,
   114→        /// Agent run ID linking this result to its parent run.
   115→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   116→        run_id: Option<String>,
   117→    },
   118→}
   119→
   120→/// User message content: plain text or multimodal array.
   121→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   122→#[serde(untagged)]
   123→pub enum MessageContent {
   124→    Text(String),
   125→    Multimodal(Vec<ContentBlock>),
   126→}
   127→
   128→/// A single block in multimodal content.
   129→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   130→#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   131→pub enum ContentBlock {
   132→    Text { text: String },
   133→    ImageUrl { image_url: ImageUrl },
   134→}
   135→
   136→/// Image URL data (for multimodal content).

   158→impl PersistedMessage {
   159→    /// Create a user message with plain text content.
   160→    pub fn user(text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
   161→        Self::User {
   162→            content: MessageContent::Text(text.into()),
   163→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   164→            audio: None,
   165→            channel: None,
   166→            seq: None,
   167→            run_id: None,
   168→        }
   169→    }
   170→
   171→    /// Create a user message with plain text and channel metadata.
   172→    pub fn user_with_channel(text: impl Into<String>, channel: serde_json::Value) -> Self {
   173→        Self::User {
   174→            content: MessageContent::Text(text.into()),
   175→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   176→            audio: None,
   177→            channel: Some(channel),
   178→            seq: None,
   179→            run_id: None,
   180→        }
   181→    }
   182→
   183→    /// Create a user message with multimodal content.
   184→    pub fn user_multimodal(blocks: Vec<ContentBlock>) -> Self {

   187→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),

   189→            channel: None,
   190→            seq: None,
   191→            run_id: None,
   192→        }

   195→    /// Create a user message with multimodal content and channel metadata.
   196→    pub fn user_multimodal_with_channel(

   205→            seq: None,
   206→            run_id: None,

   209→
   210→    /// Create an assistant message with token usage and model info.
   211→    pub fn assistant(
   212→        text: impl Into<String>,
   213→        model: impl Into<String>,
   214→        provider: impl Into<String>,
   215→        input_tokens: u32,
   216→        output_tokens: u32,
   217→        audio: Option<String>,
   218→    ) -> Self {
   219→        Self::Assistant {
   220→            content: text.into(),
   221→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   222→            model: Some(model.into()),
   223→            provider: Some(provider.into()),
   224→            input_tokens: Some(input_tokens),
   225→            output_tokens: Some(output_tokens),
   226→            duration_ms: None,
   227→            request_input_tokens: Some(input_tokens),
   228→            request_output_tokens: Some(output_tokens),
   229→            tool_calls: None,
   230→            reasoning: None,
   231→            llm_api_response: None,
   232→            audio,
   233→            seq: None,
   234→            run_id: None,
   235→        }
   236→    }
   237→
   238→    /// Create a system message (e.g., for error display).
   239→    pub fn system(text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {

   241→            content: text.into(),
   242→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   243→        }
   244→    }
   245→
   246→    /// Create a notice message shown in UI but skipped from model context.

   253→
   254→    /// Create a tool result message.
   255→    pub fn tool(tool_call_id: impl Into<String>, content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {

   263→    /// Create a tool execution result message.

   275→            arguments,
   276→            success,
   277→            result,

   279→            reasoning: None,

   293→        reasoning: Option<String>,

   296→            tool_call_id: tool_call_id.into(),
   297→            tool_name: tool_name.into(),
   298→            arguments,
   299→            success,
   300→            result,
   301→            error,
   302→            reasoning,
   303→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   304→            run_id: None,
   305→        }

   307→
   308→    /// Create a tool result message with a run ID linking it to its agent run.
   309→    pub fn tool_result_with_run_id(
   310→        tool_call_id: impl Into<String>,
   311→        tool_name: impl Into<String>,
   312→        arguments: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   313→        success: bool,
   314→        result: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   315→        error: Option<String>,
   316→        run_id: impl Into<String>,
   317→    ) -> Self {
   318→        Self::ToolResult {
   319→            tool_call_id: tool_call_id.into(),
   320→            tool_name: tool_name.into(),
   321→            arguments,
   322→            success,
   323→            result,
   324→            error,
   325→            reasoning: None,
   326→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   327→            run_id: Some(run_id.into()),
   328→        }
   329→    }
   330→
   331→    /// Convert to JSON value for storage.
   332→    ///
   333→    /// This cannot fail because `PersistedMessage` only contains types with
   334→    /// infallible serialization (strings, numbers, booleans, vecs, options).
   335→    #[allow(clippy::expect_used)]
   336→    pub fn to_value(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
   337→        serde_json::to_value(self).expect("PersistedMessage serialization cannot fail")
   338→    }
   339→}

   357→fn now_ms() -> u64 {
   358→    std::time::SystemTime::now()
   359→        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
   360→        .unwrap_or_default()
   361→        .as_millis() as u64
   362→}

Perfect! Now I have the complete picture. Let me write a comprehensive summary of my findings:

## Summary

Now I have the complete picture. Here's the clear explanation:

---

## Full Analysis

### Two different flows, two different behaviors

**Flow 1 — Within a single agent run** (works correctly):
- `run_agent_loop_streaming()` in `crates/agents/src/runner.rs` maintains an in-memory `messages: Vec<ChatMessage>` 
- When the LLM requests tool calls, the runner executes them and appends `ChatMessage::tool(id, result)` at lines 1105/1761
- The loop re-sends all messages (including tool results) on the next LLM iteration
- This works perfectly — within a single run, the LLM sees all its tool outputs

**Flow 2 — Across user turns** (broken):
- After a run completes, tool results are persisted to the session store as `PersistedMessage::ToolResult` (`crates/chat/src/lib.rs:5131`)
- When the user sends the **next message**, history is loaded from the store and converted via `values_to_chat_messages()` (`crates/agents/src/model.rs:190`)
- Line 270: `"tool_result" => continue` — **all tool results are dropped**
- The LLM receives: `[system, user, assistant(with tool_calls), user, ...]` — the tool result messages between assistant and user are **missing**

### Why "agents are extremely dumb"

The LLM sees its own assistant message saying "I'll call tool X" with `tool_calls`, but the result is gone. From the LLM's perspective, it made a tool call that vanished. Most models interpret this as a hallucination and distrust their own prior output. They either:
- Apologize and say they hallucinated
- Re-do the tool calls (wasting iterations)
- Start confidently hallucinating results instead of calling tools

### Why "prefix caching doesn't work"

This is the subtler point. LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) cache prompt prefixes — if the first N tokens of a new request match a recent request, those tokens are served from cache (cheaper, faster).

In a correct conversation, turn 2 would send:
```
[system, user₁, assistant₁(tool_calls), tool_result₁, assistant₁_final, user₂]
```

The prefix `[system, user₁, assistant₁(tool_calls), tool_result₁, assistant₁_final]` is identical to what was sent in turn 1, so the provider can cache-hit on that prefix.

But because `tool_result` is dropped, turn 2 actually sends:
```
[system, user₁, assistant₁(tool_calls), user₂]
```

This doesn't match turn 1's message sequence at all (which had `tool_result₁` after the assistant message). So the provider **cannot** reuse its prefix cache — every turn is treated as a fresh prompt, paying full price for all input tokens.

### The irony

The `full_context()` debug endpoint (line 4557-4722) **already has the correct fix** — it converts `tool_result` → `tool` before passing through `values_to_chat_messages()` (lines 4676-4701). But the actual chat path at line 6124 never does this conversion. The fix that exists in the debug view was never applied to the real chat path.

### The fix

At `crates/agents/src/model.rs:268-270`, replace the `continue` with a conversion to `ChatMessage::Tool`, mirroring what `full_context()` already does:

```rust
"tool_result" => {
    let tool_call_id = val["tool_call_id"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
    let content = if let Some(err) = val["error"].as_str() {
        format!("Error: {err}")
    } else if let Some(result) = val.get("result") {
        result.to_string()
    } else {
        String::new()
    };
    messages.push(ChatMessage::tool(tool_call_id, content));
},
```

This is the same logic already proven in `full_context()` at lines 4689-4695. Want me to implement it?

Compiles cleanly. Here's a summary of what was changed:

### Changes

1. **`crates/agents/src/model.rs:268-280`** — The core fix. Instead of skipping `tool_result` messages with `continue`, they're now converted to `ChatMessage::Tool` with the `tool_call_id` and a content string (either `"Error: ..."` from the `error` field, or the serialized `result`). This ensures the LLM sees its own tool outputs across turns.

2. **`crates/chat/src/lib.rs:4676-4707`** — Removed the now-redundant `tool_result` → `tool` conversion in `full_context()`. Since `values_to_chat_messages()` handles this natively now, the debug endpoint gets the same behavior for free without duplicating the logic.

3. **`crates/agents/src/model.rs` (tests)** — Added two tests: one for successful tool results and one for error tool results, verifying they produce correct `ChatMessage::Tool` messages.

The Codex bot is right. For the `/sh` path:
1. `tool_result` is persisted (line 5104) 
2. Then an assistant message with `tool_calls: None` is persisted (line 2886)

So in the history it's: `[..., user, tool_result, assistant(no tool_calls), ...]`. There's no assistant message with a matching `tool_calls` entry before the `tool_result`. This would create an orphan `tool` message that LLM APIs reject.

The fix should track which `tool_call_id`s appeared in assistant messages and only emit `ChatMessage::Tool` for matched ones.

I need to: (1) collect tool_call IDs from assistant messages in a first pass, then (2) only emit tool messages for matched IDs. But since this is a single-pass loop, I'll collect IDs as we go — assistant messages always come before their tool results.
